metadata.title: TextField
metadata.description: Lets users enter or edit text within a single-line input. Use to collect short, free-form information from users.

properties.description: A hint as to the intended content of the field. When set to on (the default), this property indicates that the field should support autofill, but you do not have any more semantic information on the intended contents. When set to off, you are indicating that this field contains sensitive information, or contents that are never saved, like one-time codes. Alternatively, you can provide value which describes the specific data you would like to be entered into this field during autofill.
The default value for the field.

Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user. This will also be exposed to screen reader users.
Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.
Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.
The type of icon to be displayed in the field.
A unique identifier for the element.
Content to use as the field label.
Changes the visibility of the component's label. visible: the label is visible to all users.exclusive: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.
Specifies the maximum number of characters allowed.
Specifies the min number of characters allowed.
An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.
A short hint that describes the expected value of the field.

A value to be displayed immediately before the editable portion of the field. This is useful for displaying an implied part of the value, such as "https://" or "+353". This cannot be edited by the user, and it isn't included in the value of the field. It may not be displayed until the user has interacted with the input. For example, an inline label may take the place of the prefix until the user focuses the input.
The field cannot be edited by the user. It is focusable will be announced by screen readers.

Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the error property.

A value to be displayed immediately after the editable portion of the field. This is useful for displaying an implied part of the value, such as "@shopify.com", or "%". This cannot be edited by the user, and it isn't included in the value of the field. It may not be displayed until the user has interacted with the input. For example, an inline label may take the place of the suffix until the user focuses the input.
The current value for the field. If omitted, the field will be empty.

examples.name: Main example
examples.html_code:
<s-text-field
  label="Store name"
  value="Jaded Pixel"
  placeholder="Become a merchant"
></s-text-field>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.text_field :store_name, label: "Store name", value: "Jaded Pixel", placeholder: "Become a merchant" %>

examples.name: Basic usage
examples.description: Demonstrates a simple text input field for entering a store name with autocomplete turned off, providing a clean and straightforward input experience.

examples.name: With icon
examples.description: Showcases a text field enhanced with a search icon and a placeholder, creating a visually intuitive input for searching products.

examples.name: Specific error messages for merchant context
examples.description: Demonstrates the importance of providing clear, actionable, and context-specific error messages that guide merchants toward correct input and understanding.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <!-- Generic error (avoid) -->
  <s-text-field label="Product weight" error="Invalid value"></s-text-field>

  <!-- Specific error (preferred) -->
  <s-text-field
    label="Product weight"
    error="Weight must be greater than 0 and less than 500 pounds for shipping calculations"
  ></s-text-field>

  <!-- Business rule error -->
  <s-text-field
    label="SKU"
    error="SKU 'TSHIRT-001' already exists. SKUs must be unique across all products."
  ></s-text-field>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <!-- Generic error (avoid) -->
  <%= form.text_field :product_weight, label: "Product weight", error: "Invalid value" %>

  <!-- Specific error (preferred) -->
  <%= form.text_field :product_weight, label: "Product weight", error: "Weight must be greater than 0 and less than 500 pounds for shipping calculations" %>

  <!-- Business rule error -->
  <%= form.text_field :sku, label: "SKU", error: "SKU 'TSHIRT-001' already exists. SKUs must be unique across all products." %>
</s-stack>

examples.name: Required field with validation
examples.description: Illustrates a text field marked as required, ensuring that users must provide input before form submission, with built-in validation support.

examples.name: With prefix and suffix
examples.description: Displays text field usage with prefix and suffix
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="small">
  <s-text-field label="Phone number" prefix="+03" />
  <s-text-field
    label="Credit Card Number"
    value="1234 5678 9012 3456"
    suffix="VISA"
  />
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="small">
  <%= form.text_field :phone_number, label: "Phone number", prefix: "+03" %>
  <%= form.text_field :credit_card_number, label: "Credit Card Number", value: "1234 5678 9012 3456", suffix: "VISA" %>
</s-stack>

examples.name: With accessory
examples.description: Demonstrates the flexibility of adding interactive elements like buttons to text fields, enabling immediate actions based on the entered input.
examples.html_code:
<s-tooltip id="info-tooltip">This is info tooltip</s-tooltip>
<s-text-field label="Discount code">
  <s-icon slot="accessory" interestFor="info-tooltip" type="info" />
</s-text-field>
examples.erb_code:
<s-tooltip id="info-tooltip">This is info tooltip</s-tooltip>
<%= form.text_field :discount_code, label: "Discount code" do %>
  <s-icon slot="accessory" interestfor="info-tooltip" type="info"></s-icon>
<% end %>
